channel.title: NYT > Arts > Music
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Kendrick Lamar’s Protégé Baby Keem Tells the Whole Story, Warts and All
item.description: The 25-year-old rapper and producer knows he’s benefited from his cousin’s support. But the path to his autobiographical album, “Casino,” was his alone.
item.dc:creator: Ross Scarano and Clement Pascal
item.media:credit: Clement Pascal for The New York Times
item.media:description: It has taken Baby Keem until now to feel open and skilled enough to pound his own tale of hardship, perseverance and extraordinary opportunity into a pointed piece of art.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the Live Nation Antitrust Trial
item.description: A jury found that the concert giant operated as a monopoly, a verdict that could have major reverberations in the music industry.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario
item.media:description: A judge will now determine damages and remedies in the case, which could involve divestments or a breakup of the company.

item.title: What Does an American Orchestra Need in a Conductor?
item.description: At a challenging time for American orchestras, the question of what a community wants from a music director has become more urgent.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney
item.media:credit: Brendan Bannon for The New York Times
item.media:description: JoAnn Falletta, the longtime conductor of the Buffalo Philharmonic Orchestra, said she has come to believe that an orchestra leader needs to be a visible presence both at the podium and in the community.

item.title: Live Nation and Ticketmaster Illegally Monopolized Ticketing Market, Jury Finds
item.description: In a verdict that could have far-reaching consequences in the music industry, the live colossus that includes Ticketmaster was found to have violated antitrust laws.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario
item.media:description: Live Nation faced allegations of antitrust violations in court even though the federal government settled with the company soon after the proceedings began.

item.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:description: The body of a teenage girl was found in a car belonging to the singer D4vd.

item.title: Moon Rocks! 9 Lunar Songs for the Artemis Crew
item.description: Hear from David Bowie, Neko Case, Arthur Russell and more artists inspired by our poetic, mysterious satellite.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz
item.media:description: David Bowie, one of rock’s premier aliens.

item.title: Can ‘Michael’ Help Restore Jackson’s Image? His Estate Is Banking on It.
item.description: A new biopic is the latest move in the Jackson estate’s posthumous — and lucrative — rehabilitation campaign.
item.dc:creator: Mark Binelli

item.title: Sofia Isella’s Dark Pop Is Poetic, Feminist and Right on Time
item.description: The 21-year-old singer, songwriter and producer often cakes herself in dirt — a representation of the cultural filth she sings and chants about. Her new EP is out Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Horn and Chantal Anderson
item.media:credit: Chantal Anderson for The New York Times

item.title: In Indianapolis, a New Contemporary Art Museum Comes With a D.J.
item.description: The 40,000-square-foot space, housed in a former dairy barn, aims to upend expectations of what an art museum can be.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Lee Klafczynski
item.media:description: From left, Sylvia Thomas, a poet, writer and musician; the sculptor Bryn Jackson; and the artist India Hines.

item.title: Oasis, Phil Collins and Sade to Join Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Billy Idol, Luther Vandross and Wu-Tang Clan will also be inducted, while New Edition, Mariah Carey and Melissa Etheridge failed to make the final cut.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and Museum
item.media:credit: Sammy Kogan/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press

item.title: 11 Best Coachella 2026 Looks: Justin Bieber, Addison Rae and More
item.description: The fashion at the festival is rarely rooted in reality. That’s what makes it fun.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Rotunno
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:description: FKA Twigs’s performance attire included a chain-mail hood that she removed to reveal tightly braided red hair.

item.title: South Korea Can’t Build Enough Stadiums to Host Its K-Pop Stars
item.description: South Korea doesn’t have enough arenas to show off its own A-list talent. That’s why BTS began its world tour in a once-neglected, 22-year-old soccer stadium.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: Fans gathering outside Goyang Stadium ahead of BTS’s world tour opener in Goyang, South Korea, last week.

item.title: Police Arrest Woman Accused of Stalking Lindsey Buckingham of Fleetwood Mac
item.description: The guitarist, Lindsey Buckingham, had obtained a restraining order against the woman two years ago, but the police said she continued to follow him in March.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Chad Batka for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lindsey Buckingham performing in New York in 2011. A woman who is accused of stalking the former Fleetwood Mac guitarist was arrested over the weekend in Indiana.

item.title: Kanye West’s Comeback: Sellouts, a Canceled Festival and Big Questions
item.description: Attempting a comeback after years of antisemitic statements, the artist formerly known as Kanye West is facing consequences while trying to navigate a test of his own making.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica

item.title: ‘Innocence’ at the Met Opera Transforms With Backstage Magic
item.description: The set of Kaija Saariaho’s opera “Innocence” transforms with astonishing speed. It takes a big, nimble team to make that possible.
item.dc:creator: Darryn King and Vincent Tullo
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times

item.title: In Minneapolis, Following Prince’s ‘Purple Trail’
item.description: This year is the 10th anniversary of the artist’s death. We made a pilgrimage to the city where he lived and worked.
item.dc:creator: David Farley
item.media:credit: Caroline Yang for The New York Times
item.media:description: For fans of Prince, Minneapolis, the artist’s hometown, is an essential destination. Above, a mural of Prince, painted in 2022 by the street artist Hiero Veiga, in downtown Minneapolis.

item.title: ‘Titaníque’ on Broadway: Wild ‘Titanic’ Parody, With Celine Dion as Kooky Guide
item.description: This “Titanic” parody fueled by Dion’s hits, silly ad-libs and pop culture references had the humblest of beginnings. Now the show has docked on Broadway.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

item.title: Sabrina Carpenter Apologizes for Mocking Arabic Call at Coachella
item.description: “I don’t like it,” the pop singer had said from the festival stage, after mistaking a celebratory Middle Eastern cry as a form of yodeling.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:description: Sabrina Carpenter performing at the Coachella music festival in Indio, Calif., on Friday.

item.title: Jailed for Love Songs? Yes, and Still Singing.
item.description: Nguyen Van Loc was persecuted for performing Hanoi’s old romantic music during (and after) the Vietnam War. He can’t stop. He won’t stop.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave and Linh Pham
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times

item.title: Lady Gaga and Doechii, the Strokes: 8 Songs We’re Talking About This Week
item.description: Lady Gaga and Doechii strike a pose, the Strokes side-eye consumption and Kelela confronts a crumbling relationship.
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles
item.media:description: The Strokes released their first single from “Reality Awaits,” the band’s first album since 2020, which is due in June and produced by Rick Rubin.

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa’s Complicated Legacy
item.description: The tributes to one of hip-hop’s founders were complicated by allegations of sexual abuse made against him in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:credit: Lisa Haun/Michael Ochs Archives, via Getty Images

item.title: Review: A George Lewis Premiere at the New York Philharmonic
item.description: George Lewis’s new piece brought in the percussion and piano quartet Yarn/Wire, in an evening that also featured the soprano Golda Schultz.
item.dc:creator: Seth Colter Walls

item.title: Hilde Limondjian, Met Museum’s Longtime Concert Impresario, Dies at 89
item.description: She gave New York debuts to artists like Cecilia Bartoli and Peter Serkin, and introduced new music by Philip Glass and others.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:description: Hilde Limondjian in 2003. Her excellent taste was complemented by her innovative use of the vast resources of the Metropolitan Museum of Art.

item.title: Is a Big Album Dropping? You Might Want to Watch the Road.
item.description: A study found that traffic fatalities increased in the United States by nearly 15 percent on the same days as the biggest album releases.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: Researchers from Harvard Medical School studied driver behavior data and found more smartphone use, and a lot more distraction, on days when the likes of Taylor Swift, Bad Bunny and Kendrick Lamar dropped new music.

item.title: Protoje, One of Reggae’s Premier Ambassadors, Doubles Down on His Roots
item.description: The musician’s latest album, “The Art of Acceptance,” features stars of the genre (Damian and Stephen Marley) and fresh updates on nostalgic sounds.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Meschino
item.media:credit: Elliott Verdier for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We started recording the album with the lyrics, ‘Rastafari teach I how to forgive, I’ve been learning how to live and let live,’” Protoje said.
